report.headline: Waking up heavy or headachy? Today’s signal rundown
report.quick_read: Middle East, East Asia, Southern Europe, the Desert Southwest US, and the Caribbean show the strongest sampled signals today. Space weather sits at a moderate backdrop, with current readings calmer than the day’s peaks. Earth resonance measurements aren’t available today.

regional_watch.selection_method: At least two fixed anchors must support the same driver.
regional_watch.copy:
Middle East: Samples from Riyadh, Tehran, and Istanbul show pressure swings with heat and rapid temperature shifts. Some people may notice head or sinus pressure or fatigue.

East Asia: Poor air quality was sampled across Beijing, Seoul, and Tokyo, alongside pressure swings in parts of the region. Breathing irritation or headaches can be more noticeable for some people.

Southern Europe: Madrid, Rome, and Athens sampled heat with rapid temperature shifts. Some people may feel fatigue or lower exercise tolerance.

Desert Southwest US: Phoenix and Las Vegas sampled heat with quick temperature changes. Some people may notice fatigue or lower exercise tolerance.

Caribbean: San Juan, Havana, and Santo Domingo sampled persistent heat and humidity. Some people may notice fatigue or sleep disruption.

space_watch.copy: Geomagnetic activity is moderate today. The day’s peak Kp reached about 3.7, while the current Kp is near 2. The day’s lowest north–south solar field dipped around -8 nT and is near -0.3 nT now. Solar wind averaged about 472 km/s and is currently near 462 km/s with density around 9.5/cm³. A cataloged CME count alone does not establish an Earth impact.

earth_signal.health_language: Possible effects may use 'may', 'can', or 'some people notice' without an appended disclaimer.
earth_signal.copy: Schumann resonance and ULF measurements aren’t available for public use today.

items.title: Orange forest fire notification in France

major_events.selection_method: Fresh yellow-or-higher GDACS/USGS events, deduplicated and capped.
major_events.copy:
- Wildfire: Orange forest fire notification in France (started 2026-07-14)
- Earthquake: 34 km WSW of Sarangani, Philippines (yellow, 2026-07-14)

platform_copy.facebook:
Middle East, East Asia, Southern Europe, the Desert Southwest US, and the Caribbean show the strongest sampled signals today. Space weather sits at a moderate backdrop, with current readings calmer than the day’s peaks. Earth resonance measurements aren’t available today.

Regional Watch
- Middle East: Samples from Riyadh, Tehran, and Istanbul point to pressure swings, heat, and rapid temperature shifts. Some people may notice head or sinus pressure or fatigue under these conditions.
- East Asia: Poor air quality was sampled across Beijing, Seoul, and Tokyo, with added pressure swings in parts of the region. Breathing irritation or headaches can be more noticeable for some people.
- Southern Europe: Madrid, Rome, and Athens sampled heat with rapid temperature shifts. Some people may feel fatigue or lower exercise tolerance.
- Desert Southwest US: Phoenix and Las Vegas sampled heat with quick temperature changes. Some people may notice fatigue or lower exercise tolerance.
- Caribbean: San Juan, Havana, and Santo Domingo sampled persistent heat and humidity. Some people may notice fatigue or sleep disruption.

Space Watch
Geomagnetic activity holds a moderate signal today. The day’s peak Kp reached about 3.7, while the current Kp is near 2. The day’s lowest north–south solar field dipped to around -8 nT; it’s near -0.3 nT now. Solar wind averaged near 472 km/s today and is currently about 462 km/s with density near 9.5/cm³. A cataloged CME count alone does not establish an Earth impact.

Earth Signal
Schumann resonance and ULF readings aren’t available for public use today.

Major Events
- Wildfire: Orange forest fire notification in France (started 2026-07-14)
- Earthquake: 34 km WSW of Sarangani, Philippines (yellow, 2026-07-14)

Full report: gaiaeyes.com
Personalized patterns: gaiaeyes.com/app
platform_copy.instagram: Feeling heavy or headachy today? Strongest regional signals were sampled in the Middle East (pressure swings and heat), East Asia (poor air in Beijing, Seoul, Tokyo), and Southern Europe (heat with rapid shifts). The Desert Southwest US and the Caribbean also show notable heat signals. Space weather is moderate overall, with current geomagnetic levels calmer than today’s peaks. Earth resonance readings aren’t available today. Full daily report: gaiaeyes.com
platform_copy.voiceover: Feeling a little heavy or headachy? Here’s today’s signal rundown. Strongest regional signals were sampled in the Middle East, East Asia, and Southern Europe, with heat, pressure swings, and poor air leading the mix. The Desert Southwest US and the Caribbean also light up for heat. In space, the backdrop is moderate: today’s geomagnetic peak eased to quieter current levels. Earth resonance readings aren’t available today. Stay aware of what you notice around you.
